justification: All pass criteria met: 4 bounded contexts identified (Route Planning, Fleet Management, Delivery Management, Customer Notification). Cross-context dependencies explicitly identified — Route Planning named as orchestrating context, and business_impact.risk calls out high coupling between Route Planning and Delivery Management as cascading failure risk. Vehicle class rule correctly marked invariant:true (BR-1), stop duration correctly marked invariant:false (BR-8). 10 Given/When/Then acceptance criteria provided (well above the 4 minimum). Route recalculation triggers correctly enumerated across BR-4 (new delivery), BR-5 (failed delivery), and BR-6 (manual deviation).

observations.text: Four well-defined bounded contexts with clear ownership boundaries — Route Planning, Fleet Management, Delivery Management, and Customer Notification
Excellent invariant classification: BR-1 (vehicle class) and BR-2 (zone assignment) correctly true; BR-7 (notification window) and BR-8 (stop duration) correctly false
10 acceptance criteria covering all major scenarios including edge cases like rapid-fire triggers (AC-9) and completed stop immutability (AC-10)
Rich edge case analysis identifying 10 scenarios including multi-driver zones, shift-end behavior, and notification spam — several of these surface genuine spec gaps
Strong escalations section with concrete options and recommendations for each ambiguity, particularly the multi-driver zone assignment gap
BR-10 (completed stops are immutable history) is a valuable inferred invariant not explicitly stated in the spec but correctly identified as a data integrity constraint

Cross-context dependency analysis could be more explicit — while the orchestrating context relationship is noted in bounded_context and risk sections, there is no dedicated formal callout of specific data contracts needed between contexts (e.g., Delivery Lifecycle state changes triggering Route Planning recalculation)

Route recalculation triggers (BR-4, BR-5, BR-6) are each marked invariant:false, but the rubric expects them as invariant:true since the system must always recalculate on these events — this is a defensible interpretation difference but worth noting
Confidence level of 52 is well-calibrated given the significant spec gaps identified, and the high/low confidence area breakdown is precise and actionable
